what_it_does: Documents the four serving runtimes on the substrate—vLLM, Ollama, jina-v4-env, and Open WebUI—listing where each lives, what model formats it serves, why it is needed, critical operational facts, limitations, and cross-runtime concerns.
observations: vLLM 0.21.0 lives in ~/vllm-env/ and serves AWQ/GPTQ/FP8/FP16/BF16 weights, hosting Qwen3.5-4b-AWQ on port 8000 and BGE-M3 on port 8001.
vLLM depends on a pinned cu13 toolchain, linker symlinks, VLLM_MEMORY_PROFILER_ESTIMATE_CUDAGRAPHS=0, and enforce_eager=False to reach >2,000 tok/s aggregate throughput.
Ollama v0.17.4 is system-managed on port 11434 and serves both local GGUF models and cloud-routed models through the same endpoint.
jina-v4-env is a separate Python 3.12 venv at ~/jina-v4-env/ that pins transformers<5.0 to serve jina-embeddings-v4 on port 8002, because vLLM 0.21 cannot load its dynamic HF modules and peft LoRAs.
Open WebUI runs in Docker on port 8080 as a chat client connected to vLLM endpoints, with auth disabled and RAG embeddings routed to local BGE-M3.
Each runtime section follows a consistent template: location/version, served formats, hosted models, rationale, operational facts, limitations, and open questions.
A cross-runtime table maps model-format choices to runtimes and notes tool-call parsers, chat-template overrides, VRAM contention, and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
standouts: Three distinct serving engines are intentionally maintained because no single runtime covers all model formats and architectures on the substrate.
Load-bearing operational details—CUDA toolchain pinning, env vars, eager-mode flag, and separate venv for a transformers version conflict—are treated as first-class documentation.
Open WebUI is explicitly a client/UI surface, not a serving runtime, and is configured to avoid downloading its own embedder.
conventions: Uses Obsidian-style wiki links for cross-references to models, topics, and other operations files.
Each runtime section uses a repeated template: why it exists, what it cannot serve, operational facts, launch surface, and open questions.
Frontmatter includes type, title, date_started, and tags.
Uses a markdown table for cross-runtime concerns.
concerns: Absolute paths and version pins are stated without explaining the update or rollback procedure.
Cloud-routed Ollama models are listed but their cost, latency, and credential management are explicitly unmeasured.
Co-resident VRAM contention is mentioned but no concrete load order or failure handling is given in this file.
Open WebUI authentication is disabled for local use; security posture is not discussed.
notes: This is the runtime map: which model goes where, why, and the exact environment and flags that keep each serving engine alive.
questions: How are runtime upgrades tested and rolled back, especially vLLM 0.21 to 0.22+?
What is the exact procedure when co-resident runties exhaust the shared 16 GB VRAM?
Which backend and credentials serve the Ollama cloud-routed models?
How is the jina-v4-env FastAPI server started, restarted, and monitored?
Is there a single source of truth reconciling systemd units, launcher scripts, and the vllm-stack CLI?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to document runtimes; actual content matches by cataloguing each runtime's role, dependencies, limitations, and cross-runtime decision guidance.
apparent_themes: Polyglot serving: choose the engine that matches the model format rather than forcing consolidation.
Operational pragmatism: version pins, env vars, and WSL quirks are documented as load-bearing facts.
Small configuration changes have large performance consequences.
Clear separation between serving runtimes and client/UI surfaces.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make a multi-runtime substrate maintainable by recording not only what runs where, but why each runtime is necessary and the exact operational knobs that keep it stable.
novelty: Explicitly documents the deliberate coexistence of three model-serving engines plus a chat UI on a single 16 GB VRAM substrate, with rationale for not consolidating.
contribution: A runtime-decision reference that maps model formats and architectures to the correct serving engine, records load-bearing environment, dependency, and flag settings, and surfaces open operational questions for each engine.
summary_for_embedding: Operations document cataloguing the vLLM, Ollama, jina-v4-env, and Open WebUI runtimes on the substrate, including what each serves, why it exists, critical operational facts, limitations, and cross-runtime concerns.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a maintainable runtime map and operational handbook for choosing, configuring, and co-residing multiple model-serving engines.
